notes: This is an experimenter-facing Sussex visual profile.
The companion compiles the approved visual envelope, sphere-radius, and particle-size mode values into showcase_active_runtime_config_json before upload.
The companion also exposes a simplified tracer wrapper and translates it into the existing particle-engine tracer fields during hotload export.
Coupling, drivers, splines, cycle multipliers, routing, and scene topology stay locked.

intent: Adjust only the approved Sussex visual envelope minimum and maximum values, the approved oblateness endpoint limits, and the simplified Sussex tracer wrapper while leaving coupling and all other non-visual runtime behavior unchanged.

systemDynamics.summary: These controls are visual envelope bounds plus approved sphere-deformation oblateness endpoint values, shared sphere-radius limits, particle-size interpretation mode, and a simplified tracer wrapper. The Sussex runtime uses the existing oscillator-phase drivers and existing envelope curves, and the companion derives tracer copies-per-second from lifetime and tracers-per-oscillator so the underlying particle-engine behavior stays internally consistent.

info.effect: Turns the particle-engine tracer trail system on or off while keeping the rest of the Sussex shell unchanged.
info.increaseLooksLike: Tracer trails render behind the oscillator particles using the existing AKD tracer implementation.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The main oscillator particles remain, but the trailing tracer history disappears entirely.
tradeoffs: Disabling tracers is the fastest way to remove trail overdraw without changing the other Sussex visuals.
Enabling tracers restores the existing tracer behavior and uses the hidden fixed defaults below for the non-researcher knobs.

info.effect: Controls how long each tracer snapshot remains alive before it fades out of the ring buffer.
info.increaseLooksLike: Trails stay visible longer and feel smoother and more continuous.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Trails decay faster and feel shorter and more staccato.
tradeoffs: Longer lifetimes increase the number of simultaneously alive tracers for the same per-oscillator budget.
Short lifetimes can make the shell feel cleaner, but they reduce temporal continuity.

info.effect: Controls how many tracer slots are allocated per oscillator and therefore how many snapshots can be alive per oscillator at once.
info.increaseLooksLike: Each oscillator keeps a denser, longer visible tracer history.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Each oscillator shows fewer trailing snapshots and the trail looks sparser.
tradeoffs: Higher counts increase tracer memory and overdraw cost.
The companion keeps emission density stable by deriving copies-per-second from this count and the tracer lifetime.

info.effect: Turns the stretched shell deformation model on or off.
info.increaseLooksLike: The sphere can deform and stretch instead of staying perfectly round.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The sphere stays rigid and undeformed even while the other particle effects continue running.
tradeoffs: Turning deformation off reduces one of the strongest shape cues in the Sussex scene.
Turning deformation on keeps the intended Sussex shell geometry active.

info.effect: Controls the lowest oblateness value reached across the normalized sphere-deformation curve.
info.increaseLooksLike: The low-radius state keeps more perpendicular width instead of collapsing inward as much.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The low-radius state becomes more pinched and elongated along the deformation axis.
tradeoffs: Higher values make the shell feel rounder and calmer at the low end of the radius cycle.
Very low values can make the trough state look overly squeezed or stylized.

info.effect: Controls the highest oblateness value reached across the normalized sphere-deformation curve.
info.increaseLooksLike: The high-radius state keeps more perpendicular width and reads as rounder for the fixed Sussex axis profile.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The expanded state stays more pinched and stretched along the deformation axis.
tradeoffs: Higher values reduce the apparent stretch at the top of the radius cycle.
Very low values can make the shell look unnaturally narrow even when fully expanded.

info.effect: Controls the smallest breathing-driven sphere radius reached by the shared radius channel.
info.increaseLooksLike: The shell never contracts as small, so the calmest breathing state stays visibly larger.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The shell can contract further and the low-radius phase feels tighter and more compressed.
tradeoffs: Higher minimum radius reduces contrast between small and large breathing phases.
When particle size is relative to radius, raising the minimum radius also raises the minimum world-space particle size.

info.effect: Controls the largest breathing-driven sphere radius reached by the shared radius channel.
info.increaseLooksLike: The expanded shell gets larger and the breathing crest feels more spacious.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The expanded shell stays tighter and the breathing crest feels more contained.
tradeoffs: Higher maximum radius makes the large phase more dominant in the room.
When particle size is relative to radius, raising the maximum radius also raises the peak world-space particle size.

info.effect: Chooses whether particle size limits are interpreted as fractions of the current sphere radius or as fixed world units.
info.increaseLooksLike: When enabled, particles scale up and down with the breathing-driven sphere size.
info.decreaseLooksLike: When disabled, particles keep fixed world-space sizes even while the sphere radius changes.
tradeoffs: Relative mode preserves the baseline Sussex coupling between shell size and particle size.
Raw mode can make particle-size edits easier to reason about in absolute units, but it decouples size from the breathing-driven shell radius.

info.effect: Controls the smallest particle size reached at the low points of the existing size envelope.
info.increaseLooksLike: The field stays thicker and less fine-grained even at troughs.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The field allows finer particles and stronger contrast against the peak size.
tradeoffs: Higher values reduce the visual contrast between small and large phases.
The numeric meaning depends on the mode above: fractions of sphere radius when enabled, fixed world units when disabled.

info.effect: Controls the largest particle size reached at the peak of the existing size envelope.
info.increaseLooksLike: Particles become larger and visually heavier at the peak.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Peak particles stay finer and the scene feels lighter.
tradeoffs: Higher values increase overdraw and visual dominance risk.
The numeric meaning depends on the mode above: fractions of sphere radius when enabled, fixed world units when disabled.

info.effect: Controls the smallest depth-wave amplitude reached across the existing depth-wave envelope.
info.increaseLooksLike: Even the low phase keeps more wave motion and depth texture.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The low phase flattens out more and the shell looks calmer between peaks.
tradeoffs: Higher values reduce the contrast between quiet and active depth-wave phases.
Very low values can make the shell feel too static at the trough.

info.effect: Controls the strongest depth-wave amplitude reached at the top of the existing depth-wave envelope.
info.increaseLooksLike: The shell gets more visibly rippled and spatially layered at the peak.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Peak wave motion stays subtler and the shell reads as smoother.
tradeoffs: Higher values can make the shell feel busier and visually noisier.
Very low values reduce the sense of depth-wave motion almost entirely.

info.effect: Controls the lowest alpha reached by the transparency envelope.
info.increaseLooksLike: Particles remain more consistently visible through the full cycle.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Particles can fade more strongly at the low end of the transparency cycle.
tradeoffs: Higher values reduce softness and fade contrast.
Very low values can make parts of the field disappear too aggressively.

info.effect: Controls the highest alpha reached by the transparency envelope.
info.increaseLooksLike: Particles reach full opacity more readily at the top of the cycle.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Even the peak state remains softer and more translucent.
tradeoffs: Higher values can make the field feel denser and more occluding.
Very low values can reduce visual presence even at the peak.

info.effect: Controls the lowest global saturation reached across the saturation envelope.
info.increaseLooksLike: Colors stay richer even at the muted part of the cycle.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The low phase becomes more washed out and subdued.
tradeoffs: Higher values reduce muted-versus-rich contrast.
Very low values can make the scene feel drained or flat.

info.effect: Controls the highest global saturation reached across the saturation envelope.
info.increaseLooksLike: Colors become more vivid at the top of the cycle.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The scene stays more restrained and less chromatically intense.
tradeoffs: Higher values can make the image feel harsher or more synthetic.
Very low values limit expressive color contrast.

info.effect: Controls the dimmest global brightness reached across the brightness envelope.
info.increaseLooksLike: The scene stays more luminous even in its darker phase.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The low phase becomes dimmer and more contrasty against the peak.
tradeoffs: Higher values reduce dark-versus-bright contrast.
Very low values can make the trough phase feel underlit.

info.effect: Controls the brightest global brightness reached across the brightness envelope.
info.increaseLooksLike: The peak phase becomes brighter and more radiant.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The whole scene stays softer and more restrained at the top end.
tradeoffs: Higher values can make the peak phase feel harsher or blow out detail.
Very low values limit brightness contrast and overall punch.

info.effect: Controls the smallest orbit radius multiplier reached across the orbit-distance envelope.
info.increaseLooksLike: Particles keep more lateral offset even in the calmer orbit phase.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The low orbit phase pulls particles closer to their base positions.
tradeoffs: Higher values reduce the contrast between tight and wide orbit phases.
Very low values can make the low orbit phase look almost stationary.

info.effect: Controls the widest orbit radius multiplier reached at the peak of the orbit-distance envelope.
info.increaseLooksLike: Particles travel farther from their base positions and the shell feels more expansive.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Peak orbit spread stays tighter and more compact.
tradeoffs: Higher values can make the shell feel more chaotic and spatially diffuse.
Very low values flatten the orbit effect and reduce motion richness.

crossFieldValidation: controls.sphere_radius_min.value must be less than or equal to controls.sphere_radius_max.value
controls.particle_size_min.value must be less than or equal to controls.particle_size_max.value
controls.transparency_min.value must be less than or equal to controls.transparency_max.value
controls.saturation_min.value must be less than or equal to controls.saturation_max.value
controls.brightness_min.value must be less than or equal to controls.brightness_max.value

compilerHints.outputContract: Compile the approved visual envelope, sphere-radius, and particle-size mode fields into the runtime json payload, then append the simplified tracer wrapper as flat hotload keys that translate back into the existing particle-engine tracer parameters before upload. Do not upload this source file directly to the headset as the final hotload artifact.
